premises-NL: P2. If a student does not receive training, then they do not have a research foundation.
P3. At least one student has completed a course.
P4. At least one student is certified.
P5. If a student does not receive training, then they do not have pedagogical skills.
P6. If a student does not receive training, then they are not certified.
P7. If a student lacks pedagogical skills, then they cannot teach.
P8. If a student is not certified, then they cannot teach.
P9. If a student has pedagogical skills, then they have received training.
P10. All students have pedagogical skills.
P11. If a student cannot teach, then they lack a research foundation.
P12. If at least one student is certified, then if a lack of research foundation leads to not being certified, then not being able to teach leads to a lack of research foundation.
P13. If a student has completed a course, then the above logical rule holds true.
question-NL: Based on the above premises, which statement can be inferred?

choices.A: If a student is not certified, then the student cannot teach, and it is not true that if a student cannot teach, then the student lacks a research foundation.
choices.B: If it is true that a student who is not certified cannot teach, then it is also true that a student who cannot teach lacks a research foundation.
choices.C: If it is true that a student who cannot teach lacks a research foundation, then it is also true that a student who is not certified cannot teach.
choices.D: It is not true that if a student who is not certified cannot teach, then a student who cannot teach lacks a research foundation.
